title: Komm Susser Tod
wikitext:
[]: Komm Susser Tod             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Sun Sep 11, 2005 8:32 am Am I the only person that becomes both depressed and happy when you hear this song? It's really strange to describe this song...

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Sun Sep 11, 2005 8:48 am Yeah, when I first heard it (before I'd seen EoE) I was like, "Wow, what a crappy song!" And now when I hear it, I wanna cry. It's depressing, but the tone is kinda...happy. o_O;

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 11, 2005 9:01 am It's a happy song about wanting to die. Of course it's going to cause cognitive dissonance - like this word: GREEN Though the last time I heard it, I just felt bewildered - it was being used as background to a slideshow of a SFWJ party, full of happy cosplayers

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sun Sep 11, 2005 9:33 am I broke into laughter the first time I heared it during EoE. So very appropriate...

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Sun Sep 11, 2005 9:38 am I cried. Like a S/A shipper. Well, I didn't -literally- cry, but I was freaking out.

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Sun Sep 11, 2005 11:02 am I like it. To be honest, I didn't notice it in EoE until about the fourth time I saw the movie, or so... :) But I only like the first part, the "It all returns to nothing, it all comes tumbling down, tumbling, down, tumbling down" (or whatever) thing is a bit 'boring' to me... So, well... It fits better to the movie, but as a song I only like the first part.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 11, 2005 11:36 am The tune stuck in my mind after first seeing EoE (and the first time hearing it) - probably by the association of the jaunty melody with the shock of the first strangulation scene, and then the Instrumentality images - though I didn't catch the words that time around.

[]:             Quote Post by PieRat » Sun Sep 11, 2005 11:41 am Ehh in what part of EoE is the song? can't find it :(

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 11, 2005 11:46 am [code:1]Shinji (weakly - getting to his feet): Help me... Somebody... Somebody help me... Help me... Help me... Me... Help ME!! (throws the table aside) Don't leave me alone! Don't abandon me! Don't kill me! (picks up a chair and throws it at Asuka's feet) Asuka (coldly): ... No... (Shinji strangles Asuka - Komm Susser Tod (Come Sweet Death) begins to play) [/code:1]

[]:             Quote Post by PieRat » Sun Sep 11, 2005 11:52 am ok thanks Mr.Tines

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Sun Sep 11, 2005 12:34 pm The song is beutiful. I listen to it near everyday... several times a day. It actually makes me feel better when I am sad or feel mono when I am happy. And I like being mono.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Sun Sep 11, 2005 1:10 pm Mr. Tines wrote: [code:1]Shinji (weakly - getting to his feet): Help me... Somebody... Somebody help me... Help me... Help me... Me... Help ME!! (throws the table aside) Don't leave me alone! Don't abandon me! Don't kill me! (picks up a chair and throws it at Asuka's feet) Asuka (coldly): ... No... (Shinji strangles Asuka - Komm Susser Tod (Come Sweet Death) begins to play) [/code:1]

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Sun Sep 11, 2005 3:54 pm Ahahahahaha... When I first saw that scene, I glomped Sachiel and started crying like a little baby. That song cracks me up. Like, I like it, but I only play it in the confines of my own room, where no one can hear me and I dont feel stupid. Its kind of scary.

[]:             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Sun Sep 11, 2005 5:16 pm Tsu-chan wrote: The song is beutiful. I listen to it near everyday... several times a day. It actually makes me feel better when I am sad or feel mono when I am happy. And I like being mono.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Sep 11, 2005 7:40 pm That song is what set me on the path to collecting Eva soundtracks.

[]:             Quote Post by Hiromakuta » Mon Sep 12, 2005 4:56 am ThatÂ´s a song I rarely hear. ItÂ´s oxymoronic and has that "deep" feeling. Indeed a song to hear once a week, or else it would be pretty much spoil the "effect" that the song has.

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Mon Sep 12, 2005 2:36 pm Hiromakuta wrote: ThatÂ´s a song I rarely hear. ItÂ´s oxymoronic and has that "deep" feeling. Indeed a song to hear once a week, or else it would be pretty much spoil the "effect" that the song has.

[]:             Quote Post by Hiromakuta » Mon Sep 12, 2005 2:48 pm Tsu-chan wrote: Hiromakuta wrote: ThatÂ´s a song I rarely hear. ItÂ´s oxymoronic and has that "deep" feeling. Indeed a song to hear once a week, or else it would be pretty much spoil the "effect" that the song has.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Mon Sep 12, 2005 7:59 pm Hiromakuta wrote: Tsu-chan wrote: Hiromakuta wrote: ThatÂ´s a song I rarely hear. ItÂ´s oxymoronic and has that "deep" feeling. Indeed a song to hear once a week, or else it would be pretty much spoil the "effect" that the song has.

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Wed Sep 14, 2005 2:23 am "naaa na na, na-na-na-naaa. na-na-na-naaa, heeey jude." surprisingly, i'm not the only one who felt that way.
